pimHumanDescription.en: The name of the publisher identifier scheme.
The URI of the publisher identifier scheme.
Uniquely identifies the publisher, according to various schemes.
The URI leading to a page provided by the funder for more information about the award (grant).
Name of the funding provider.
The code assigned by the funder to a sponsored award (grant).
The human readable title or name of the award (grant).
The URI of the subject identifier scheme.
The name of the subject scheme or classification code or authority if one is used.
The URI of the subject term.
The classification code used for the subject term in the subject scheme.
Specific date.
The type of date.
Specific information about the date, if appropriate.
The URI of the name identifier scheme.
The name of the affiliation identifier scheme
The full name of the creator.
The name of the name identifier scheme.
The surname or last name of the creator.
The organizational or institutional affiliation of the creator.
Uniquely identifies the organizational affiliation of the creator.
The URI of the affiliation identifier scheme.
Uniquely identifies an individual or legal entity, according to various schemes.
The type of name. Allowed values are Organizational and Personal.
The personal or first name of the creator.
The type of the RelatedIdentifier. Value from controlled list https://datacite-metadata-schema.readthedocs.io/en/4.6/appendices/appendix-1/relatedIdentifierType/.
The name of the scheme. Use only with hasMetadata or IsMetadataFor relation type.
The URI of the relatedMetadataScheme. The name of the scheme. Use only with hasMetadata or IsMetadataFor relation type.
The general type of the related resource. Value from controlled list https://datacite-metadata-schema.readthedocs.io/en/4.6/properties/resourcetype/#a.
The type of the relatedMetadataScheme, linked with the schemeURI.  Use only with hasMetadata or IsMetadataFor relation type.

Description of the relationship of the resource being registered (A) and the related resource (B). Value from controlled vocabulary https://datacite-metadata-schema.readthedocs.io/en/4.6/appendices/appendix-1/relationType/.
Northern latitudinal dimension of box.
Western longitudinal dimension of box.
Eastern longitudinal dimension of box.
Southern latitudinal dimension of box.
The Identifier is a unique string that identifies a resource. In DataCite, this MUST be a DIgital Object Identifier (DOI). The format should be 10.21384/foo.
A name or title by which a resource is known. May be the title of a dataset or the name of a piece of software or an instrument.

The year when the data was or will be made publicly available. In the case of resources such as software or dynamic data where there may be multiple releases in one year, include the Date property and sub-properties (dateType/dateInformation) to provide more information about the publication or release date details.
Size (e.g., bytes, pages, inches, etc.) or duration (extent), e.g., hours, minutes, days, etc., of a resource.
The version number of the resource.
The primary language of the resource.
Technical format of the resource.
A description of the resource. Gets the value from controlled list https://datacite-metadata-schema.readthedocs.io/en/4.6/appendices/appendix-1/resourceTypeGeneral/.
The type of name. Allowed values are Organizational and Personal.
The organizational or institutional affiliation of the contributor.
The name of the name identifier scheme.
The URI of the name identifier scheme.
URI of the affiliation identifier scheme.
The name of the affiliation identifier scheme.
The personal or first name of the contributor.
The surname or last name of the contributor.
The full name of the contributor.
The type of contributor of the resource. Value from controlled list https://datacite-metadata-schema.readthedocs.io/en/4.6/appendices/appendix-1/contributorType/.
Uniquely identifies an individual or legal entity, according to various schemes.
Description of a geographic location.
Text describing the resource.
The type of the Description. Value from controlled list https://datacite-metadata-schema.readthedocs.io/en/4.6/appendices/appendix-1/descriptionType/.
Relation to the type of the description.
The name of the scheme.
The URI of the license.
A short, standardized version of the license name.
The URI of the rightsIdentifierScheme.
For any bound area that is larger than half the earth, define a (random) point inside.
A point location in a polygon.
Longitudinal dimension of point.
Latitudinal dimension of point.
The type of the Alternate identifier.
The type of the funderIdentifier.
The URI of the funder identifier scheme.
The general type of the related item. Value from controlled list https://datacite-metadata-schema.readthedocs.io/en/4.6/appendices/appendix-1/resourceTypeGeneral/.
The institution or person responsible for creating the related resource.
Title of the related item.
The identifier for the related item.
The year when the item was or will be made publicly available.
The name of the entity that holds, archives, publishes prints, distributes, releases, issues, or produces the resource.

Description of the relationship of the resource being registered (A) and the related item (B). Value from controlled list https://datacite-metadata-schema.readthedocs.io/en/4.6/appendices/appendix-1/relationType/.
Volume of the related item.
Issue number or name of the related item.
Number of the resource within the related item, e.g., report number or article number.
First page of the resource within the related item, e.g., of the chapter, article, or conference paper in proceedings.
Edition or version of the related item.
An institution or person identified as contributing to the development of the resource. If multiple contributors are identified, this sub-property may be repeated for each contributor.
Last page of the resource within the related item, e.g., of the chapter, article, or conference paper in proceedings.

The name of the entity that holds, archives, publishes, prints, distributes, releases, issues, or produces the resource. This property will be used to formulate the citation, so consider the prominence of the role.
Information about financial support (funding) for the resource being registered.
Subject, keyword, classification code, or key phrase describing the resource.
Different dates relevant to the work.

The main researchers involved in producing the data, or the authors of the publication, in priority order. For instruments this is the manufacturer or developer of the instrument. To supply multiple creators, repeat this property.
dentifiers of related resources. These must be globally unique identifiers.
The spatial limits of a box.
Root element of a single record. This wrapper element represents root element taken from XML implementation and is not defined in the DataCite DOI standard. RDF also needs a root element.

he institution or person responsible for collecting, managing, distributing, or otherwise contributing to the development of the resource. To supply multiple contributors, repeat this property.

For software, if there is an alternate entity that “holds, archives, publishes, prints, distributes, releases, issues, or produces” the code, use the contributorType “HostingInstitution” for the code repository.

For instruments, if there is an institution responsible for the management of the instrument (for example, the legal owner, the operator, or an institute providing access to the instrument), use the contributorType “HostingInstitution” for the owner of the instrument.
Spatial region or named place where the data was gathered or about which the data is focused.
All additional information that does not fit in any of the other categories. May be used for technical information or detailed information associated with a scientific instrument.
Any rights information for this resource. The property may be repeated to record complex rights characteristics.
The type of the description.
A drawn polygon area, defined by a set of points and lines connecting the points in a closed chain.
A point location in space.

An identifier other than the primary Identifier applied to the resource being registered. This may be any alphanumeric string which is unique within its domain of issue. May be used for local identifiers, a serial number of an instrument or an inventory number. The AlternateIdentifier should be an additional identifier for the same instance of the resource (i.e., same location, same file).
Uniquely identifies a funding entity, according to various types.
Information about a resource related to the one being registered.
